name: Georgia (United States)
description: Three horizontal bands — red, white, red — of equal width. A blue canton occupies the upper hoist, charged with the gold coat of arms of Georgia (an arch on three pillars defended by a soldier) surrounded by thirteen white five-pointed stars in a ring, with the words 'IN GOD WE TRUST' below the arms.
history: Georgia's pre-2001 flag prominently featured the Confederate Battle Flag (added in 1956). After controversy, the legislature replaced it in 2001 with a transitional flag. The current 'Stars and Bars'-derived flag was adopted on 8 May 2003, taking effect after a 2004 referendum confirmed it as the permanent design. The motto 'IN GOD WE TRUST' was added in 2001.

features.symbolism: the red and white bars echo the first national flag of the Confederacy ('Stars and Bars'); the design closely follows that flag

features.role: blue canton with state arms and stars
the coat of arms of Georgia
features.symbolism: an arch supported by three pillars representing the three branches of government, with a soldier between the pillars defending the constitution; the pillars are entwined with banners reading 'WISDOM', 'JUSTICE', and 'MODERATION'; date 1776 below

features.role: ring of stars around the arms
features.symbolism: Georgia as one of the original thirteen colonies

features.role: the words 'IN GOD WE TRUST' beneath the arms
